nodes.title: Introduction to Sociology
nodes.description: Fundamental principles and problems in the sociological analysis of the structure and dynamics of modern society.

nodes.title: Sociology of Disaster
nodes.description: Examination of disasters using a sociological, critical lens. Analysis of research, theories, and current debates in the field of disaster sociology. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Elements of Statistics
nodes.description: Basic statistical concepts, methods, and applications, including correlation, regression, confidence intervals, and hypothesis tests. Prerequisites: Two years of high school algebra.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of high school algebra. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Asian-American Experiences
nodes.description: An overview of the socio-historical conditions of people of Asian descent in the United States, along with an examination of contemporary issues. Cross-listed with: CRES 1885.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Social Theory
nodes.description: Classical sociological theory including Marx, Weber, Durkheim, and Mead, as well as DuBois and early female theorists such as Martineau. Reading and writing intensive. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; three additional hours of Sociology; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: three additional hours of Sociology; minimum Sophomore standing. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Sociology of Law
nodes.description: Analysis of sociocultural structure of the legal institution and its relationships to other institutions: the social organization of the legal profession, lawmaking, and the courts. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing. Declared Law & Society minors may substitute SOC 1210 for other prerequisite coursework in Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing. Declared Law & Society minors may substitute SOC 1210 for other prerequisite coursework in Sociology. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Crime
nodes.description: Analysis of the nature and types of behavior that violates law, the mechanisms for defining such behaviors as criminal, and their causes and consequences. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Sociology; Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: U.S. Food Policy and Politics
nodes.description: Provides a systems perspective on U.S. food policies and politics across the food system. Focuses on understanding the U.S. food policy process, policymakers, stakeholders, issues, goals and feedbacks between food policy and politics. Prerequisite: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross-listed with: NFS 2113.
nodes.other_requirements: Cross-listed with NFS 2113. Prerequisites enforced by the system: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross listed with NFS 2113A.

nodes.title: Basic Statistical Methods 1
nodes.description: Foundational course for students taking further quantitative courses. Exploratory data analysis, probability distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing. Introductory regression, experimentation, contingency tables, and nonparametrics. Computer software used. Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: FYS: Education, Pwr &Privilege
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar focused on specific themes and/or disciplinary perspectives. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking. First-year seminars are frequently organized to meet one of the disciplinary Catamount Core requirements. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: The Holocaust
nodes.description: Study of the background, events, and aftermath of the Holocaust in Nazi Germany and Europe under German control. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Sociology of Sexualities
nodes.description: Examination of the social construction of sexuality with emphasis on theories, concepts, and cultural ramifications of a range of sexual practices and identities. Cross-listed with: GSWS 1785.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Three hours of Sociology; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Sociology; Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Family as Social Institution
nodes.description: Examination of the institution of the American family in cross-cultural and historical perspective. Theories and research on family continuity, change, and institutional relationships explored. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: LASP:Asian-American Experience
nodes.description: Intensive course in a broad disciplinary area (humanities, social sciences, arts, or natural sciences). Part of an integrated first-year experience in which students take 2-4 classes exploring aesthetic, humanistic, social, linguistic, environmental, or scientific issues. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Co-requisite: Enrollment in the appropriate Liberal Arts Scholars Program.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: Enrollment in the appropriate Liberal Arts Scholars Program. LASP Social Science Scholars only. Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Race Relations in the US
nodes.description: Introduction to the sociological analysis of race and ethnicity, with a focus on the histories of racial and ethnic groups in the United States. Credit not awarded for both SOC 1370 and SOC 1372 or CRES 1883. Cross-listed with: CRES 1881.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both SOC 1370 and SOC 1372 or CRES 1883. Cross-listed with: CRES 1881. Open to degree and PACE students. This course fulfills the D1 and social science requirements.

nodes.title: Global Deviance
nodes.description: Studies different theoretical approaches to deviance and social control, empirical patterns of deviant behaviors, and temporal, spatial, and cultural variations in these patterns, in a global context. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology or Global and Regional Studies.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing, PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Social Research Methods
nodes.description: Introduction to research methods in social science. Includes examination of research design, measurement, data collection, data analysis, and the presentation and theoretical interpretation of research findings. Prerequisites: STAT 1050, STAT 1110, STAT 1410, or higher; three hours of Sociology or Political Science; minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: POLS 2800.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: U.S. Food Policy and Politics
nodes.description: Provides a systems perspective on U.S. food policies and politics across the food system. Focuses on understanding the U.S. food policy process, policymakers, stakeholders, issues, goals and feedbacks between food policy and politics. Prerequisites: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross-listed with: FS 2010.

nodes.title: LASP: Social Inequality
nodes.description: Intensive course in a broad disciplinary area (humanities, social sciences, arts, or natural sciences). Part of an integrated first-year experience in which students take 2-4 classes exploring aesthetic, humanistic, social, linguistic, environmental, or scientific issues. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Co-requisite: Enrollment in the appropriate Liberal Arts Scholars Program.
nodes.other_requirements: LASP students only; Social Sciences Scholars only; Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Deviance & Social Control
nodes.description: Analysis of the causes and consequences of social behavior that violates norms. Examines patterns of deviant socialization and social organization and forms of deviance control.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Racial Inequality in the US
nodes.description: A critical analysis of racial inequality and stratification in the United States. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology or Critical Race & Ethnic Studies. Cross-listed with: CRES 2881.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Sociology or Critical Race & Ethnic Studies. Pre-reqs 3 hours of Sociology; minimum Soph standing

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Criminalized Races & Genders
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: SL:Internship Seminar in SOC
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior standing; Instructor permission; Twelve hours of sociology. First seats go to Senior Sociology majors who need the course to graduate, next they go to senior sociology minors who need the course to graduate, and next they go to junior sociology majors. You cannot register without an override.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; Instructor permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Disability as Deviance
nodes.description: Analyzes constructions of disability as deviance in current and historical contexts such as American eugenics, Nazi sterilization and medical crimes, and present national policies. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; PACE students by permission and override; Steady class attendance and participation.

nodes.title: Sociology of the Holocaust
nodes.description: Examination of the Holocaust using a sociological lens and discussion of its relevance for current social developments and events. Prerequisites: Three hours of Sociology or three hours of Jewish Studies or HST 1715 or HST 2760 or HST 2790.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Sociology or three hours of Jewish Studies or Minimum Sophomore standing PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Global Health Crises
nodes.description: Examination of contemporary and historical case studies of global health crises to understand the role of science, politics, and power in social construction of risk and health.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students. Students will be expected to attend all lectures sessions, participate actively in weekly course discussion board posts and online conversations, complete in-class exams, and engage in a group book report project.

nodes.title: Crime and the City
nodes.description: Takes an in-depth view of the varied and contested relationship between crime and cities, drawing on insights from urban and political sociology, criminology and law, and social and spatial inequalities. Covers a range of key studies in the field with an emphasis on urban ethnography in major US cities in comparative perspective with cities from around the world, including in the Global South. Prerequisites: SOC 1500; SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Statistics and Society
nodes.description: Explores how data shape public understanding of social issues while building statistical literacy. Students will learn to identify bias, consider ethical and structural influences, and apply descriptive and inferential statistics to interpret, visualize, and evaluate data across varied communities and perspectives. Credit not awarded after STAT 2000 or greater. Prerequisites: Two years High School algebra; no credit for Sophomores, Juniors, or Seniors in the mathematical and engineering sciences; credit for only one of STAT 051 and STAT 1050.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years High School algebra; no credit for Sophomores, Juniors, or Seniors in the mathematical and engineering sciences; Cannot be taken for credit after taking STAT 051 or STAT 052.

nodes.title: Self and Society
nodes.description: Analysis of the roles of sociocultural and situational factors in individual behavior and experience and the social genesis, development, and functioning of human personality. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Comparative Race & Ethnicity
nodes.description: A comparative analysis of racial and ethnic contact in the United States, Brazil, Canada, Germany, and Australia. Prerequisites: SOC 1500, and SOC 2500 or SOC 2550; or six hours in CRES; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Six hours in CRES; minimum Junior standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Modern Germany
nodes.description: Political, cultural, and social history of Germany from unification in 1871 through the Wilhemine empire, Weimar Republic, Nazi era, and postwar period. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Social Research Methods
nodes.description: Introduction to research methods in social science. Includes examination of research design, measurement, data collection, data analysis, and the presentation and theoretical interpretation of research findings. Prerequisites: STAT 1050, STAT 1110, STAT 1410, or higher; three hours of Sociology or Political Science; minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: SOC 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Population, Environment & Soc
nodes.description: Analysis of the causes and consequences of varying relationships among population size, distribution and composition, social organization, technology, and resource base. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: min Sophomore standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Food, People & Environment
nodes.description: Guides students to critically examine the production, distribution, and consumption of food, as well as related culture, power, and ecological issues. With a focus on the modern food system, students will explore the relationship between food, people, and the environment using a sociological lens. Prerequisites: (SOC 1500 and SOC 2500 or SOC 2550) or (FS 2010 or NFS 2113); minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Sociology of Religion&Ideology
nodes.description: Beliefs and value systems and their institutional arrangements, focusing on relationships between these systems and the larger social structure, in cross-cultural and historical perspective. Prerequisite: Three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore Standing. PACE students by permission and override. Regular attendance and willingness to read books and articles.
